description: Per-year column-header scan cross-referenced against curated candidate-source dictionary for currently-blocked PRISM targets. Identifies strong / proxy / weak / not_usable column candidates per target × year. Tags forbidden columns. Produces a v0.1 implementation priority list of the 3-5 safest targets to map first. The mapper is NOT modified by this audit.

candidates.rationale: Retrospective economic evaluation; correlates with material orientation but also tracks partisan thermometer
candidates.directionality: Worse-economy answer roughly co-moves with redistributionist position; verify per cycle (sign can flip during incumbent-out years).

candidates.rationale: Family income — weak correlate of material preferences
candidates.directionality: Lower income → MAT 1-2 (redistributionist). Effect is small; not load-bearing alone.

candidates.rationale: Renamed family-income column in some 2020+ releases
Stock ownership; correlates with free-market lean
Union household; left-economic correlate (also feeds moralBoundaries.class)
Deficit-reduction means (cuts vs taxes vs both)
candidates.directionality: Prefer cuts → MAT 4-5; prefer taxes → MAT 1-2.

candidates.rationale: Religion importance; weak proxy (also feeds moralBoundaries.religious)
Abortion battery — always allow as matter of choice
Immigration battery item 1 (per A2 spec) — DACA / pathway
Immigration battery item 8 (varies; e.g., English official language)
candidates.directionality: Same; one item may be `English-only` which inverts polarity.

candidates.rationale: Refugee item inside immigration battery (where present)
Muslim-related item in some cycles' immigration battery
Approve/disapprove of how Congress is doing — institutional-trust proxy
Sporadic 'politicians should compromise' items in 2016/2020 (column name varies)
Pessimistic economic framing correlates with zero-sum lens (weak)
Trade items in some cycles ('other countries gain at our expense')
Feldman-Stenner child-rearing battery is rare in CCES; mostly ANES
candidates.directionality: Verify item exists; default ANES path.

candidates.rationale: CCES does not ship the Feldman-Stenner authoritarianism battery in standard form; mapping must wait for ANES integration
'Government for the few or for all' — system-trust proxy
News-interest level — doesn't directly probe epistemic style but provides a weak engagement-adjacent prior
candidates.directionality: n/a — fallback prior near-uniform is appropriate.

candidates.rationale: News-source items where present (which outlets respondent trusts) — partial signal
candidates.directionality: Source pattern → empiricist/intuitionist split; needs prototype calibration.

candidates.rationale: Where a 'science-of-X' or 'experts' item exists (rare in CCES standard)
CCES does not directly probe epistemic-style category; surveys would need an EPS-prototype battery to discriminate cleanly

Surveys do not directly probe aesthetic-style category. Acknowledged in the spec as inherently fallback for the v0/v1 mapper — would need a deliberately-designed AES battery (statesman vs technocrat vs pastoral vs authentic vs fighter vs visionary cues) that does not currently exist in CCES.
Immigration restrictiveness composite — direct national-boundary signal
Anti-Muslim-ban → low salience; pro-ban → high salience
English-only-language item where present — national-identity signal
Affirmative-action items where present (column name varies by cycle)
candidates.directionality: Pro-AA → high salience for race-conscious respondents.

candidates.rationale: Sporadic Me Too / sexual-harassment items in 2018+ (column name varies)

forbidden_columns.reason: Presidential vote choice (modern cycle convention CC{Y2}_410[a]) — would create vote-prediction circularity
House / Senate vote choice (CC{Y2}_411 / 412 / 413) — same circularity
Candidate feeling thermometer batteries (CC{Y2}_320*, CC{Y2}_330*) — vote-affect circularity for position nodes
Pre-election turnout intent — engagement-only input; forbidden as a position-node feature
Self-reported turnout — engagement-only input; forbidden for position nodes
Validated turnout — engagement-only input; forbidden for position nodes (would cross-contaminate vote-prediction backtest)
Validated turnout — engagement-only input; forbidden for position nodes
Validated turnout — engagement-only input; forbidden for position nodes

Party ID 7-pt — allowed only as `partyIdDerived: true` input to `moralBoundaries.political_camp` salience per the mapper's design; FORBIDDEN as input to MAT/CD/CU/MOR/PRO/COM/ZS/ONT_H/ONT_S core position nodes (would make vote-prediction circular)
Party ID 3-pt — same restriction as pid7

v0_1_implementation_priority_list.reason: Abortion battery (CC{Y2}_332a-f) is well-covered in 2012/2016/2020/2024 with stable directionality across cycles. Direct issue-attitude alignment with PRISM cultural-direction. Low circularity risk.
v0_1_implementation_priority_list.sequencing_note: Ship first because the abortion battery's polarity is the most stable across cycles. Will move CD off uniform-prior fallback to ~5-6 real-signal targets per row. Cross-cycle holdout possible (4 cycles × 6 items).

v0_1_implementation_priority_list.reason: Immigration battery (CC{Y2}_331_1..8) is the next-most-covered standard battery. Direct issue-attitude alignment with cultural-uniformity. Mostly stable polarity (one English-only item may flip).
v0_1_implementation_priority_list.sequencing_note: Ship together with target #4 (moralBoundaries.national) because both consume the same battery — saves one column-resolver pass.

v0_1_implementation_priority_list.reason: Federal-spending battery (CC{Y2}_337_*) + ACA item (CC{Y2}_351I) + minimum-wage item (CC{Y2}_351K) provide direct issue-attitude alignment with material orientation. Already-mapped weak proxies (faminc, union, investor, no_healthins) can be dropped to secondary support.
v0_1_implementation_priority_list.sequencing_note: Verify column presence per cycle — the spec lists 2016 as the canonical cycle; 2020 likely has a similar battery, 2012/2024 may use different stems. Expect partial coverage outside 2016/2020 and document the per-cycle gap.

v0_1_implementation_priority_list.reason: Same immigration battery as CU but used as a salience composite rather than a position decoder. Co-shipping with CU is efficient. The political_camp boundary is already the only `partyIdDerived: true` channel; this fills another always-fallback boundary slot.
v0_1_implementation_priority_list.sequencing_note: Ship with #2. Salience composite = `count of restrictionist answers + intensity`. Document that respondents at extreme-pluralist (CU 4-5) also get high national-boundary salience — the boundary measures *engagement with* the dimension, not direction on it.

v0_1_implementation_priority_list.reason: Trade items (CC{Y2}_330a where present) + immigration-takes-jobs items provide a narrow but unambiguous zero-sum signal in 2016/2020. Lower priority because items are sparser; ship as a stretch goal alongside #1-#4.
v0_1_implementation_priority_list.sequencing_note: Optional in v0.1. If column presence checks fail in 2012/2024, document and skip those years rather than block the rollout.

notes.year_2008_caveat: 2008 uses opaque V### column naming (legacy YouGov scheme). The candidate dictionary's `CC{Y2}_*` patterns therefore expand to `CC08_*` which are absent. 2008 needs a separate per-year resolver pass driven by `data/cces2008/codebook-extracted.txt` to surface candidate columns.
notes.AES_acknowledged_fallback: moralBoundaries-adjacent / AES is documented in the survey-to-prism-v0 spec as inherently fallback (surveys do not directly probe aesthetic preference). v0.1 priority correctly omits AES.
notes.EPS_partial_signal: EPS would benefit from a deliberately-designed prototype battery; standard CCES news-source items provide only weak proxy signal.
notes.ONT_H_anes_path: ONT_H requires the Feldman-Stenner authoritarianism child-rearing battery, which CCES does not ship in standard form. Mapping requires ANES integration (out of v0.1 scope).
